title: Non-done columns default to FIFO (oldest first) ordering
description:
Issues in any column whose status does not belong to the Done category are displayed oldest-first (by created_at ascending) by default. This is FIFO order. It is the baseline behavior when no sort_order configuration is present.

## Rationale

FIFO is the correct default for any queue that feeds a continuous delivery process. In Vacanti's framework, aging work in active columns is a signal — the longer something has been sitting, the more likely it is to be blocking flow or accumulating risk. Oldest-first ordering makes that signal impossible to ignore: the card that needs attention most is right at the top. Any other default — priority order, recency, alphabetical — hides aging and trains teams to ignore it.

Scenario: Discovery column shows oldest issue first by default

Given several issues are in the Discovery column with different created_at timestamps
And no sort_order is configured in .kanbus.yml
When the user views the kanban board
Then the issue with the earliest created_at timestamp appears at the top of the Discovery column
And issues are ordered from oldest to newest going downward

Scenario: In Progress column shows oldest issue first by default

Given several issues are in the In Progress column with different created_at timestamps
And no sort_order is configured
When the user views the kanban board
Then the issue with the earliest created_at timestamp appears at the top of In Progress

Scenario: Blocked column shows oldest blocked issue first by default

Given several issues are in the Blocked column
And no sort_order is configured
When the user views the kanban board
Then the oldest blocked issue appears at the top of the Blocked column

Scenario: FIFO order applies to Backlog column by default

Given the Backlog column has issues with different created_at timestamps
And no sort_order is configured
When the user views the kanban board
Then the oldest issue in Backlog appears at the top
